refactor: single sidecar ETL replaces all backfill jobs and CronJob

The etl-sidecar runs inside the erigon pod with localhost RPC (no network
hop, no contention). It backfills from block 0 to chain head, then loops

- 4 parallel backfill Jobs (caused RPC contention)
- CronJob sync (redundant with sidecar)
- Python ETL ConfigMap (already removed)

- CH_URL now includes ?database=ethereum

- Bundle 1001 BBC podcasts as a gzip-compressed seed asset (167 KB) in
  app/src/main/assets/podcasts_seed.json.gz for instant display on first launch
- Add PodcastRepository.getAvailablePodcastsNow(): returns locally available

  code duplication

  1. Show local data instantly (no spinner if data exists)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix IMAGE_VERSION unset in final stage of multi-stage Docker builds (#25907)

In Docker multi-stage builds, ARG declarations are scoped to the build
stage they appear in. All affected Dockerfiles declare ARG image_version
only in the first (builder) stage but not in the final stage. Since
rsync_from_builder_stage() copies only filesystem content (not Docker
ENV metadata), ENV IMAGE_VERSION in the final stage resolves to an
empty string.

This causes container_startup.py to fail with:
  error: argument -v/--version: expected one argument

Fix: Add ARG image_version in the final stage of all 16 affected
Dockerfiles, and add missing ENV IMAGE_VERSION where needed, so the
build-time value is correctly passed through to the runtime image.

Signed-off-by: Storm Liang <stormliang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: configure renovate to restrict go.opentelemetry.io/** updates to minor versions only (#1663)

Recent `go.opentelemetry.io` patch releases have bumped their minimum Go
requirement to 1.25, breaking this project's Go 1.24 support floor.

## Changes

- **`.github/renovate.json`**: Added a `packageRules` entry that
disables `major` and `patch` update types for all
`go.opentelemetry.io/**` packages, leaving only minor version updates
enabled.

```json
{
  "description": "Restrict go.opentelemetry.io/** to minor version updates only",
  "matchPackagePrefixes": ["go.opentelemetry.io/"],
  "matchUpdateTypes": ["major", "patch"],
  "enabled": false
}
```

Make `registry` and `digest` required on lockfile `PackageDependency` (#264)

* Initial plan

* feat: make registry and digest required fields on PackageDependency in lockfile

Add `registry` and `digest` as required fields on `PackageDependency` so
that every dependency reference in the lockfile is fully pinned. Add
`Lockfile::resolve_dependency_details()` to backfill these fields from
top-level package entries after installation completes.

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add clarifying comments for empty registry/digest placeholders

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: key resolve_dependency_details by (name, version), return Result, skip versionless deps

Address review feedback:
- Key the lookup by (name, version) instead of just name to handle
  packages that share a name but differ by version.
- Return Result<(), LockfileResolveError> so callers can detect
  unresolved or duplicate entries instead of silently writing empty
  fields.
- Use filter_map to skip dependency entries that have no resolved
  version, preventing empty-version placeholders in the lockfile.

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove unnecessary clones in resolve_dependency_details

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: simplify resolve_dependency_details to strip unresolved deps

Address second round of review feedback:
- Remove LockfileResolveError (no longer needed).
- resolve_dependency_details() now silently strips dependency entries
  whose name has no matching top-level package. This correctly handles
  offline mode and skipped transitive deps without writing empty fields.
- Lookup uses name-based HashMap (no duplicate key issue since the same
  name from different registries is handled by first-wins semantics).
- No unnecessary String allocations in the lookup phase.

Match dependencies by both name and version to ensure correct
registry/digest when multiple packages share the same name but
have different versions.

Add --imports and --exports flags to `wasm registry search` (#253)

* Initial plan

* feat: add --imports and --exports flags to `wasm registry search`

Add support for searching packages by their WIT interface imports and
exports. The new SQL queries JOIN through the OCI → WIT table chain
(oci_repository → oci_manifest → wit_package → wit_world →
wit_world_import/wit_world_export) to find matching packages.

Usage:
  wasm registry search --exports wasi:http <query>
  wasm registry search --imports wasi:http <query>

docs: add dedicated pages for image resizing and quality control (#1661)

`usage.md` was accumulating too much content; image resizing and quality
control warranted their own pages to keep navigation clean.

## New pages

- **Image Resizing** (`weight: 5`) — covers the `MANAEL_ENABLE_RESIZE`
opt-in, `w`/`h`/`fit` query parameters, aspect-ratio behaviour for
partial dimension specs, and security controls
(`MANAEL_MAX_RESIZE_WIDTH`, `MANAEL_MAX_RESIZE_HEIGHT`,
`MANAEL_ALLOWED_WIDTHS`, `MANAEL_ALLOWED_HEIGHTS`)
- **Quality Control** (`weight: 6`) — covers the `q` query parameter
(universal `?q=80` and format-specific `?q=webp:80,avif:50`), clamping
rules, per-format built-in defaults (WebP 90, AVIF 60), and the
`MANAEL_DEFAULT_QUALITY` server-level override with its full resolution
priority order

Both pages are provided in English and Japanese under
`website/content/{en,ja}/docs/`.

4-channel dosing: chlorine, pH down, pH up, antifoam

- 3 servo granule hoppers: chlorine (GP13), pH down (GP12), pH up (GP11)
- 1 peristaltic pump via relay: antifoam liquid (GP10)
- Auto-dose logic: chlorine when <1ppm, pH down when >7.8, pH up when <7.2
- Antifoam on timed schedule (every 4 hours)
- Per-channel safety: individual cooldowns, daily max, amount tracking
- One-at-a-time dispensing to avoid servo power draw spikes
- Dashboard shows all 4 channels with status, counters, manual dose buttons
- JSON API extended with per-channel dispenser status
- Updated BOM (+3 servos, peristaltic pump, relay)
- Updated wiring diagram with all 4 dispensers
